style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<div style="text-align: start; font-family: &quot;Segoe UI&quot;; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;">NgRx is a <strong>reactive state‑management library</strong> for Angular based on the <strong>Redux pattern</strong>. It uses <strong>RxJS</strong> heavily and follows <strong>unidirectional data flow</strong>.</div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="30" width="950" x="-60" y="-70" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="8EFbGZOhAiZ7-bGYGHr--38" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<div style="text-align: start; font-family: &quot;Segoe UI&quot;; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><div style="line-height: 20px;">NgRx is especially useful in medium‑to‑large Angular applications where <strong>shared state</strong>, <strong>complex UI interactions</strong>, <strong>API caching</strong>, and <strong>predictability</strong> matter.</div></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="30" width="960" x="-60" y="-20" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="xDRoHF1HpzbG-9HopF0--1" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<div style="text-align: start; font-family: &quot;Segoe UI&quot;; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><p>NgRx follows the principles of Redux:</p><ol><li><strong>Single Source of Truth</strong> – One store holds all application state.</li><li><strong>State is Read‑Only</strong> – You can’t mutate it directly.</li><li><strong>State changes via Pure Reducers</strong> – Deterministic changes only.</li></ol></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="150" width="490" x="-560" y="-120" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="xDRoHF1HpzbG-9HopF0--3" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" value="<div style="text-align: start; font-family: &quot;Segoe UI&quot;; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><p>Use NgRx when:</p><p>✔ Large app with complex shared state<br>✔ Many components use the same data<br>✔ Predictability &amp; debugging matter<br>✔ You want time‑travel debugging (Redux DevTools)<br>✔ You need memoized selectors for performance<br>✔ You need centralized side‑effects handling</p></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="170" width="420" x="930" y="-130" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

import { createAction, props } from '@ngrx/store';
import { Product } from '../models/product.model';

export const loadProducts = createAction('[Product] Load Products');

export const loadProductsSuccess = createAction(
  '[Product] Load Products Success',
  props<{ products: Product[] }>()
);

export const loadProductsFailure = createAction(
  '[Product] Load Products Failure',
  props<{ error: any }>()
);

import { createReducer, on } from '@ngrx/store';
import * as ProductActions from './product.actions';
import { Product } from '../models/product.model';

export interface ProductState {
  products: Product[];
  loading: boolean;
  error: any;
}

export const initialState: ProductState = {
  products: [],
  loading: false,
  error: null
};

export const productReducer = createReducer(
  initialState,

  on(ProductActions.loadProducts, (state) => ({
    ...state,
    loading: true
  })),

  on(ProductActions.loadProductsSuccess, (state, { products }) => ({
    ...state,
    loading: false,
    products
  })),

  on(ProductActions.loadProductsFailure, (state, { error }) => ({
    ...state,
    loading: false,
    error
  }))
);

a.xlink:href:
https://app.diagrams.net/
// Dispatch actions

ngOnInit() {
  this.store.dispatch(loadProducts());
}

// Select data
products$ = this.store.select(selectAllProducts);
loading$ = this.store.select(selectLoading);

// HTML

<div *ngIf="loading$ | async">Loading...</div>
<ul>
  <li *ngFor="let p of (products$ | async)">
    {{ p.title }} - {{ p.price }}
  </li>
</ul>

div.div: NgRx is a reactive state‑management library for Angular based on the Redux pattern. It uses RxJS heavily and follows unidirectional data flow.

div.div: NgRx is especially useful in medium‑to‑large Angular applications where shared state, complex UI interactions, API caching, and predictability matter.

div.p: NgRx follows the principles of Redux:

ol.li: Single Source of Truth – One store holds all application state.
State is Read‑Only – You can’t mutate it directly.
State changes via Pure Reducers – Deterministic changes only.

div.p:
✔ Large app with complex shared state
✔ Many components use the same data
✔ Predictability & debugging matter
✔ You want time‑travel debugging (Redux DevTools)
✔ You need memoized selectors for performance
✔ You need centralized side‑effects handling
